title: Calendar group filtering — filter which groups appear in the calendar
body:
## Issue description

This child issue of the **Personal Event Ownership Epic** tracks the implementation and testing of calendar group filtering. Users must be able to toggle which groups' shared events appear in their calendar view, including the ability to show/hide personal events. Filter preferences must persist across sessions.

### Current state

**Hook (implemented):**
- `useCalendarFilter` hook persists filter state in localStorage (key `nc-calendar-filter`)
- Filter state shape: `{ personal: boolean, groups: Record<string, boolean> }`
- Default: all groups visible, personal events visible
- Provides `toggleGroup(groupId)`, `togglePersonal()`, `isGroupVisible(groupId)`, `isPersonalVisible` APIs

**UI component (implemented):**
- `GroupFilterPanel.tsx` — displays toggle switches for personal events and each group
- Groups are color-coded using `--nc-group-color-{index}` CSS variables
- Personal events toggle uses `--nc-group-color-personal` CSS variable
- Panel integrates with the calendar toolbar

**Calendar integration (implemented):**
- `Calendar.tsx` uses the filter to conditionally fetch and display events
- Personal events are fetched only when `isPersonalVisible` is true
- Group events are fetched only for groups where `isGroupVisible(groupId)` is true
- Event deduplication handles events shared to multiple visible groups

**Design system support (implemented):**
- `GROUP_COLOR_SLOT_COUNT = 8` predefined group color slots
- `groupColorVar(index)` builds CSS variable names: `--nc-group-color-0` through `--nc-group-color-7`
- `PERSONAL_COLOR_VAR = '--nc-group-color-personal'`
- All colors defined in theme tokens for both light and dark modes

**Tests (implemented):**
- `client/src/__tests__/hooks/useCalendarFilter.test.ts` — tests for the hook
- `client/src/__tests__/utils/group-colors.test.ts` — tests for color utilities

### Remaining work

- Verify filter persistence works correctly after page refresh
- Verify filter correctly handles groups that the user leaves or is removed from
- Verify new groups added after initial load are visible by default

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

1. Users can toggle visibility of personal events in the calendar
2. Users can toggle visibility of each group's shared events in the calendar
3. Filter preferences persist across sessions (localStorage)
4. New groups default to visible
5. Toggling a group off immediately hides its events from the calendar
6. Toggling a group on immediately shows its events in the calendar
7. Groups are color-coded with consistent colors across sessions
8. Personal events have a distinct color separate from group colors
9. Filter panel is accessible (keyboard navigation, ARIA labels)
10. Filter works consistently across all view modes (day, week, month, custom)

---

## Additional notes

### Key files
- Hook: `client/src/hooks/useCalendarFilter.ts`
- Panel: `client/src/pages/Calendar/GroupFilterPanel.tsx`
- Calendar integration: `client/src/pages/Calendar/Calendar.tsx`
- Color utilities: `client/src/utils/group-colors.ts`
- Design tokens: `client/src/design-system/tokens.ts` (lines 118-141)
- Theme definitions: `client/src/design-system/themes.ts` (lines 138-150)
- Hook tests: `client/src/__tests__/hooks/useCalendarFilter.test.ts`
- Color tests: `client/src/__tests__/utils/group-colors.test.ts`

### Design decisions
- localStorage chosen for simplicity — no server round-trip for filter changes
- Filter state is a simple boolean map — easy to serialize/deserialize
- Group colors are index-based (slot 0-7) to ensure consistent assignment
- Personal events have a dedicated color to visually distinguish them from group events

### Parent epic
Personal Event Ownership — Shift from Group-Owned to User-Owned Events

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Personal events toggle shows/hides personal events in the calendar
- [ ] Group toggle shows/hides that group's shared events in the calendar
- [ ] Filter preferences persist to localStorage and survive page refresh
- [ ] New groups default to visible in the filter
- [ ] Groups are assigned consistent colors (slot-based)
- [ ] Personal events use distinct personal color
- [ ] Filter works across all view modes (day, week, month, custom)
- [ ] Filter panel is accessible (keyboard, ARIA)
- [ ] Hook tests pass
- [ ] Color utility tests pass
- [ ] Calendar component tests pass with filter interactions
- [ ] All existing client tests continue to pass
- [ ] CI passes
